patch_previews.proposed_markdown_delta: Loop engineering can be framed as the design of the operating harness around an agent, not simply the wording of a single request. In that framing, the important design choices include what starts the loop, what goal it is allowed to pursue, what context and tools it may use, what checks are applied while it works, what state it can carry forward, how outcomes are reported, when retries are permitted, and when the loop must stop or escalate. This is a cautious framing of current loop-engineering commentary, not a claim that prompt design has disappeared or that the term has a settled industry definition.
